sub.dfe: to nod (with head, side to side)

entry.dfe: to put, to pour, to serve

sub.nb: only ex of 'water' with tone ¹, many other phrases all tone ²

entry.nb: il est bien précisé "sit only" (not 'stay, live')

entry.dfe: to shout, to scream, to howl

sub.nb: (m) : is it really jam rather than jaŋ? MM

entry.dfe: can (to be able to) do s.th.

entry.nb: need to check nepali (?like a child, ? cf patch)

entry.dfe: to scoop out, to dip into (itr)

sub.nb: participe passe de ¹kʰla de *¹kʰla-si MM

entry.nb: only entry of 'to do' (¹la) with tone ²

sub.nb: (t) : this (t) analysis is certainly wrong MM

entry.dfe: to tidy up a dry field after sowing
to cause to draw ?/? to erase

entry.nb: I know from comparison that this is morphophonemically |men| but we have no indication in the source MM
'min-na la-pa to cause to become soft, ripe (13.a)

entry.dfe: to whet (by hand, with whet stone)

entry.nb: it is the second ¹naː which means 'endure'. the first is 'pain' MM
this must be a tonal error for ³naŋ MM
le m/s dit : "other" sns autre précision
prob tone error for ³paŋ MM

sub.dfe: to cause to feel at ease

entry.dfe: to chatter, to babble, to prattle

sub.nb: tone and translation are wrong MM

sub.dfe: to slander s.o., to tell tales about s.o.

entry.dfe: to shave, to shear, to cut hair
to give s.o. to be married
to ripen artificially in hot bed, to dry-ferment

sub.dfe: to cause s.o. to do s.th.

entry.dfe: to clear a field (for first cultivation)
to peel (to strip skin with hands)

entry.nb: tone 4 and aspiration are incompatible prob ⁴pjaː MM
one entry with tone 1 against two with tone 2 MM

entry.dfe: to sprinkle pure water on s.o. for purification

sub.dfe: to break down the lumps in a wet field

entry.dfe: to heat up roti on the fire

entry.nb: only one ex of seŋ 'to do' with tone ¹, against dozens with tone ³
dozens of exx, all with tone ³

sub.dfe: to wash mud floors and walls with a watery mixture of cowdung and mud

entry.nb: on a aussi : ¹tai-na la-pa (màme racine que 15.5.)

sub.dfe: to slander s.o., to tell tales about s.o.

entry.dfe: to be pleased, to be happy
to fix, to put in (e..g. window frames)

entry.nb: prob 'to check the day (for auspiciousness)' MM

entry.dfe: to strike, to thresh, to pound (grain)
entry.nb: to thresh (rice and wheat) or to thresh by beating with a stick (millet, buck-wheat)

entry.dfe: to insert, to instill, to bring in
to store, to put in the right place
to season with spices fried in butter

sub.nb: (cf. 14.30 to catch in a trap). Prob typo pour /cuŋ/ MM

entry.dfe: to cut the bark off a tree
to run, to flee, to jump

sub.dfe: to leave behind, to desert, to abandon

entry.dfe: to stoke, to stir up fire
to encourage, to urge, to tempt

entry.dfe: to sit, to stay, to live, to remain
to sow (a lot at a time)
to give s.o. to be married
